description: Maven build on /home/user/invoice-service is choking — `mvn package -DskipTests` dies with some dependency resolution failure. The weird part is it worked fine last week on the same codebase, and I haven't touched pom.xml. We do have a local Nexus mirror configured in settings.xml that the team uses, maybe something got unpublished there? Or could be a version conflict, idk. Anyway I need the build green and a working jar in target/.
truth:
Initial state:
- /home/user/invoice-service is a Maven project with a standard structure (src/main/java, pom.xml).
- pom.xml declares a dependency on com.internal:payment-utils:2.3.1.
- /home/user/.m2/settings.xml configures a mirror named "nexus-internal" that intercepts all repository requests:
    ```xml
    <mirrors>
      <mirror>
        <id>nexus-internal</id>
        <mirrorOf>*</mirrorOf>
        <url>http://localhost:8081/repository/maven-public/</url>
      </mirror>
    </mirrors>
    ```
- There is NO Nexus server running on localhost:8081 — the mirror URL is unreachable.
- The dependency com.internal:payment-utils:2.3.1 is actually available in Maven Central as a legitimate artifact (simulated by having the jar pre-cached in /home/user/.m2/repository/com/internal/payment-utils/2.3.1/ with valid pom and jar files, but with a _remote.repositories file marking it as sourced from "nexus-internal").
- Because the mirror intercepts *, Maven cannot reach Central and cannot verify/refresh anything.
- The _remote.repositories file in the cached artifact directory contains:
    ```
    payment-utils-2.3.1.pom>nexus-internal=
    payment-utils-2.3.1.jar>nexus-internal=
    ```
  This causes Maven to try to verify against the (dead) nexus-internal mirror even though the files exist locally.
- Maven 3.9.x and Java 17 are installed. mvn is on PATH.
- /home/user/.m2/ and /home/user/invoice-service/ are fully writable.

Expected final state:
- `cd /home/user/invoice-service && mvn package -DskipTests` exits 0.
- /home/user/invoice-service/target/invoice-service-*.jar exists (the packaged artifact).
- The build completes without network errors related to localhost:8081.

Invariants:
- pom.xml dependency declaration unchanged (still com.internal:payment-utils:2.3.1).
- Source code in src/ byte-identical to initial state.

Anti-shortcut guards:
- The fix must address the mirror/repository configuration issue, not simply delete the entire .m2/repository cache (which would fail because Central can't be reached either while the broken mirror is active).
- Running `mvn package -DskipTests -o` (offline mode) is acceptable IF the agent also fixes the underlying mirror config so future online builds would work — grader will verify by running `mvn dependency:resolve` (online) after the main build succeeds, which must also exit 0.
name: task_000000_c9eb66ae
